name: Clara Siagian
title: Postdoctoral researcher
institution: Bartlett Development Planning Unit, University College London
bio: I examine the intersection of people, place, and politics to understand how young people navigate state interventions in the Asia-Pacific. With a focus on Indonesia, my research spans housing, urban development, and climate governance, grounded in ten years of participatory fieldwork. I have an exceptional track record of independent leadership, including AU$1.1M in funding and six peer-reviewed articles (5 in Q1; 210+ citations). My work translates complex social dynamics into actionable policy knowledge, currently supported by a prestigious postdoctoral appointment at Bartlett Development Planning Unit, University College London. Complementing my academic output, I have consulted for the World Bank and UN agencies, bringing research-based policy insights into global development agenda.
collaborate_intro: I am open to research collaboration and opportunities to engage on topics related to my work. Whether you are a fellow researcher, a practitioner, or simply curious — feel free to reach out. I would love to hear from you.
